description_long: This is a small, rustic barn with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in overlapping wooden shingles. The front facade features a single, wide hinged door made of vertical wooden planks, set within a lower section of horizontally stacked logs. The upper portion of the walls is finished with a smooth, light-colored plaster or stucco, contrasting with the rougher log base. There are minimal windows: a small square opening above the door and another on the side, both simple and utilitarian. The barn sits on a patch of grassy ground, with some outdoor elements like a well and a barrel visible nearby. The overall appearance suggests a traditional, hand-built agricultural structure, likely used for storage or sheltering animals.
description: A small, rustic barn with a steep gable roof, log lower walls, and minimal windows, set on grassy ground.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide wooden door, log base, plaster upper wall, and a small square window above the entrance.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the steep, shingled gable roof and the rectangular footprint of the barn.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the log wall construction, a small side window, and some outdoor items like a barrel and well.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the uninterrupted log wall and the expanse of the shingled roof.
attribute_siding_pattern: lower horizontal log construction with upper smooth plaster finish
attribute_door_style: single wide hinged door with vertical planks
attribute_window_arrangement: one small square window above the door and one small side window, both sparsely placed
